content: Compound interest is a powerful way your savings can grow faster over time. Unlike simple interest, which pays you only on the original amount, compound interest pays you on both your initial savings and the interest it has already earned. Think of it like a snowball rolling down a hill—it starts small but gets bigger as it picks up more snow. The longer you leave your money invested, the more it grows because you’re earning “interest on interest.” This effect may seem slow at first, but over years, it can significantly increase your savings without any extra effort from you. For a busy executive like yourself, understanding this means the earlier you start saving, the greater your financial benefit will be in the long run. Compound interest works quietly in the background, steadily increasing your wealth, making it one of the smartest tools for building financial security over time.
